displayTitle: Seeing Through Steel, Trusting the Meter
kicker: RT LEVEL I // FROM RAYS TO IMAGE

beats.narration: Welcome to radiographic testing: penetrating radiation passes through the part, imaging the whole volume and leaving a permanent record.

payload.title: RADIOGRAPHY
payload.sub: The inside of the part, without cutting it open.

beats.narration: Eighteen ninety-five: Roentgen discovers X rays. Eighteen ninety-six: Becquerel's uranium fogs plates on its own. Eighteen ninety-eight: the Curies isolate radium.
How the image forms: a void absorbs less than solid steel, so more radiation reaches the film, printing a darker spot. Differential absorption.

payload.heading: How the radiographic image forms
payload.note: LESS ABSORPTION → MORE EXPOSURE → DARKER SPOT

beats.narration: Two source families: X ray machines stop when power is cut. Gamma isotopes like iridium one ninety-two and cobalt sixty are never off, only shielded.
The rule above all: radiation gives no warning. You can't see it or feel it. Survey after every exposure. Trust only the meter.

payload.sub: The meter is the only witness you can trust.
